steps.title: Set up the slope-intercept form
steps.content: We know the line has slope $m = \frac{1}{8}$, so it has the form $y = \frac{1}{8}x + b$.

steps.title: Use the point to find the y-intercept
steps.content:
The line passes through $(40, -6)$. Substitute into the equation:
$$-6 = \frac{1}{8}(40) + b$$
Simplify: $-6 = 5 + b$, so $b = -11$.
The equation is $y = \frac{1}{8}x - 11$. That matches choice B — B is correct.
